questions.question: What is population?
questions.correct_answer: The total number of people living in a particular area
questions.explanation: Population refers to the number of people living in a country or region and is an important factor in economic and social development.

questions.question: What was India's population according to the 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: According to the Census of India 2011, the population of India was about 1,210.6 million.

questions.question: Which state has the largest population in India?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India according to Census 2011.

questions.question: What is population density?
questions.correct_answer: The number of persons living per square kilometre of land area
questions.explanation: Population density helps understand how crowded a region is by measuring people per unit area.

questions.question: What was India's population density in 2011?
questions.explanation: India’s population density increased to 382 persons per sq km according to Census 2011.

questions.question: What is population growth?
questions.correct_answer: The change in the number of inhabitants of a country over a period of time
questions.explanation: Population growth refers to the increase in population measured in absolute numbers or percentage.

questions.question: What is birth rate?
questions.correct_answer: The number of live births per thousand persons in a year
questions.explanation: Birth rate is an important factor influencing population growth.

questions.question: What is death rate?
questions.correct_answer: The number of deaths per thousand persons in a year
questions.explanation: A decline in death rate can lead to an increase in population growth.

questions.question: What is migration?
questions.correct_answer: The movement of people from one place to another
questions.explanation: Migration can occur within a country (internal) or between countries (international).

questions.question: What is the adolescent population age group?
questions.explanation: Adolescents form an important part of the population and are crucial for the future workforce.

notes.title: Importance of Population
notes.content: Population is important because people develop resources, use them, and contribute to economic and social development.

notes.title: Population Distribution
notes.content: Population distribution in India is uneven. Northern plains and coastal regions have higher population densities than mountainous or desert areas.

notes.title: Factors Affecting Population Growth
notes.content: Population growth is mainly influenced by birth rate, death rate, and migration.

notes.title: Migration and Urbanization
notes.content: Migration from rural areas to cities occurs due to employment opportunities, better education, and improved living conditions.

notes.title: National Population Policy 2000
notes.content: The policy focuses on improving family welfare, reducing infant mortality, promoting education, and controlling population growth.

facts.fact: India accounts for more than 17 percent of the world’s population.
India occupies only about 2.4 percent of the world's land area.
India became the most populous country in the world in 2023.
Adolescents make up about one-fifth of India's population.
